per_entity_results.generated: QUIC Server and Client Performance Evaluation Across Multiple Implementations
per_entity_results.reference: Performance Evaluation of QUIC Implementations with Limited Bandwidth

per_entity_results.generated: This dataset documents an experiment evaluating the performance of several QUIC server and client implementations under varying network conditions. The methodology involved automated setup of nodes, installation of software packages, compilation of QUIC libraries, and execution of client-server transfer tests with controlled bandwidth limits and file sizes.  Evaluation scripts analyzed per-run logs to determine goodput and generate visualizations summarizing the relationship between bandwidth limitations and achieved throughput across different implementations. Archive contents include build scripts, configuration files, evaluation outputs, and per-run log data.
per_entity_results.reference: This experiment executes a single file download with client-server pairs of different QUIC implementations. The filesize, the implementations, and a bandwidth limit can be set to multiple values to execute the measurements with all combinations of the parameters. The amount of repetitions can also be set to repeat the same combination n times to ensure stable results. An inbuilt evaluation script aggregates results and creates a simple plot that compares different QUIC implementations.

per_entity_results.generated: The script orchestrates the installation and compilation of several QUIC implementations, including lsquic, picoquic, quic-go, quiche, tquic, and aioquic. It manages repository cloning, version control, dependency installation via apt and cargo, and build processes for each project. The resulting executables are copied to the current directory.
per_entity_results.reference: Install Rust and Go to then build all QUIC implementations from source.

per_entity_results.generated: The script configures and launches a QUIC server with varying implementations, creating test files and certificates for secure communication. It applies bandwidth limits using traffic control and captures energy measurements during operation, synchronizing with client activity to ensure coordinated execution. Per-run logs and checksums of the test file are recorded for analysis.
per_entity_results.reference: Start a QUIC server with a given library. In advance, a testfile and a certificate is generated. If set, a bandwidth limit is applied with Linux tc. After a client signalizes a finished transfer, the server is terminated.

per_entity_results.generated: Each iteration establishes a server with a specified implementation, file size, and bandwidth limit to evaluate network performance. The script creates test content, configures TLS certificates, applies traffic shaping, and starts the server process before synchronizing with client activity and terminating cleanly. Per-run logs capture server status, certificate details, and resource utilization.
per_entity_results.reference: In each iteration, the QUIC client downloads a testfile from the QUIC server. All combinations for all values of implementation, bandwidth and filesize are run at least once, depending on the amount of repetitions set. For each run, the duration as well as logs from the respective endpoint are stored.

per_entity_results.generated: The script initiates a client-side transfer test, synchronizing with a server before and after the data exchange. It measures elapsed time for each run using timestamps and records energy consumption if available. Downloaded files are checksummed and uploaded as logs.
per_entity_results.reference: Start a QUIC client to download a file from a QUIC server. After a complete download, the file is hashed and the resulting value is stored to a file.
